assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies this as admission webhook compatibility issue after Kubernetes upgrade, stating webhooks become incompatible with new API server version

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides commands to list validating and mutating admission webhooks, and specifically find cert-manager webhooks

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Provides immediate fix by patching webhook to remove webhook rules, and alternative to delete webhooks entirely

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Includes kubectl delete commands for both validating and mutating admission webhooks in the alternative quick recovery section

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Clearly states this is temporary emergency fix and explains it allows pods to be created while cert-manager is fixed

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions checking cert-manager compatibility matrix and provides link to supported releases documentation

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Recommends upgrading to v1.16.1 for k8s 1.32 support, but expectation asks for 1.30 support - there's a version mismatch in the expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Does not mention failurePolicy configuration option as an alternative to webhook deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides verification commands including test pod creation and checking cert-manager resources

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Does not mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Does not discuss failurePolicy configuration or its risks and temporary nature
